message:
So I was digging through a closet last week looking for board games—you know, trying to do the whole "phones down, family time" thing—and I found this ancient battery-powered radio from like 2005. Dust everywhere. But it got me thinking: when's the last time I actually *tested* my smoke alarms? Not just assumed they work, but actually pressed the button?

Turns out one was dead. Just dead. And I'd walked past it a hundred times.

It's funny how the low-tech stuff—the stuff that doesn't need WiFi or notifications—is exactly what we forget about until we remember. A working smoke alarm doesn't buzz with reminders. It just... sits there, ready.

Anyway, I replaced the batteries and actually made a little escape plan sketch with my kids (they thought it was like planning a heist, which, fine—if it gets them thinking about it). Found this refresher pretty helpful too.

When was the last time you actually tested yours? No judgment—I'm still the person who forgets to flip the calendar. 😅
